message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Importance</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>The SARS-CoV-2 non-spike structural proteins of nucleocapsid (N), membrane (M) and envelope (E) are critical in the host cell interferon response and memory T-cell immunity and have been grossly overlooked in the development of COVID vaccines.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objective</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>To determine the safety and immunogenicity of UB-612, a multitope vaccine containing S1-RBD-sFc protein and rationally-designed promiscuous peptides representing sequence-conserved Th and CTL epitopes on the Sarbecovirus nucleocapsid (N), membrane (M) and spike (S2) proteins.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Design, setting and participants</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    UB-612 booster vaccination was conducted in Taiwan. A UB-612 booster dose was administered 6-8 months post-2
                    <jats:sup>nd</jats:sup>
                    dose in 1,478 vaccinees from 3,844 healthy participants (aged 18-85 years) who completed a prior placebo (saline)-controlled, randomized, observer-blind, multi-center Phase-2 primary 2-dose series (100-μg per dose; 28-day apart) of UB-612. The interim safety and immunogenicity were evaluated until 14 days post-booster.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Exposure</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Vaccination with a booster 3
                    <jats:sup>rd</jats:sup>
                    -dose (100-μg) of UB-612 vaccine.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Main outcomes and measures</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Solicited local and systemic AEs were recorded for seven days in the e-diaries of study participants, while skin allergic reactions were recorded for fourteen days. The primary immunogenicity endpoints included viral-neutralizing antibodies against live SARS-CoV-2 wild-type (WT, Wuhan strain) and live Delta variant (VNT
                    <jats:sub>50</jats:sub>
                    ), and against pseudovirus WT and Omicron variant (pVNT
                    <jats:sub>50</jats:sub>
                    ). The secondary immunogenicity endpoints included anti-S1-RBD IgG antibody, S1-RBD:ACE2 binding inhibition, and T-cell responses by ELISpot and Intracellular Staining.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    No post-booster vaccine-related serious adverse events were recorded. The most common solicited adverse events were injection site pain and fatigue, mostly mild and transient. The UB-612 booster prompted a striking upsurge of neutralizing antibodies against live WT Wuhan strain (VNT
                    <jats:sub>50</jats:sub>
                    , 1,711) associated with unusually high cross-neutralization against Delta variant (VNT
                    <jats:sub>50</jats:sub>
                    , 1,282); and similarly with a strong effect against pseudovirus WT (pVNT
                    <jats:sub>50,</jats:sub>
                    6,245) and Omicron variant (pVNT
                    <jats:sub>50</jats:sub>
                    , 1,196). Upon boosting, the lower VNT
                    <jats:sub>50</jats:sub>
                    and pVNT
                    <jats:sub>50</jats:sub>
                    titers of the elderly in the primary series were uplifted to the same levels as those of the young adults. The UB-612 also induced robust, durable VoC antigen-specific Th1-oriented (IFN-γ
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    -) responses along with CD8
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    T-cell (CD107a
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    -Granzyme B
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    ) cytotoxicity.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusions and relevance</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>With a pronounced cross-reactive booster effect on B- and T-cell immunity, UB-612 may serve as a universal vaccine booster for comprehensive immunity enhancement against emergent VoCs.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Trial registration</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    [ClinicalTrials.gov:
                    <jats:ext-link xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" ext-link-type="clintrialgov" xlink:href="NCT04773067">NCT04773067</jats:ext-link>
                    ]
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>KEY POINTS</jats:title>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>Question</jats:title>
                    <jats:p>Facing ever-emergent SARS-CoV-2 variants and long-haul COVID, can composition-updated new vaccines be constructed capable of inducing striking, durable booster-recalled B/T-immunity to prevent infection by VoCs?</jats:p>
                  </jats:sec>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>Findings</jats:title>
                    <jats:p>
                      In a Phase-2 extension study, a booster dose of UB-612 multitope protein-peptide vaccine prompted high viral-neutralizing titers against live wild-type virus (VNT
                      <jats:sub>50</jats:sub>
                      , 1,711), Delta variant (VNT
                      <jats:sub>50</jats:sub>
                      , 1,282); pseudovirus wild-type (pVNT
                      <jats:sub>50</jats:sub>
                      , 6,245) and Omicron variant (pVNT
                      <jats:sub>50</jats:sub>
                      , 1,196). Robust, durable Th1-IFNγ
                      <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                      responses and CD8
                      <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                      T cell-(CD107a
                      <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                      -Granzyme B
                      <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                      ) cytotoxic activity were both observed.
                    </jats:p>
                  </jats:sec>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>Meaning</jats:title>
                    <jats:p>UB-612 RBD-sFc vaccine armed with T cell immunity-promoting conserved N, M and S2 Th/CTL epitope peptides may serve as a universal vaccine to fend off new VoCs.</jats:p>
                  </jats:sec>
                </jats:sec>
title: UB-612, a Multitope Universal Vaccine Eliciting a Balanced B and T Cell Immunity against SARS-CoV-2 Variants of Concern
